title: NOC 12200 – Accounting Technicians and Bookkeepers | GTR Immigration
description: Accounting Technicians and Bookkeepers play a vital role in maintaining financial accuracy and regulatory compliance for Canadian businesses across all industries. Classified under NOC 12200, this occupation benefits from sustained nationwide demand and offers practical immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.major_group: 122 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations
classification.minor_group: 1220 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations

duties: Maintain financial records using manual and computerized systems
Post journal entries and reconcile accounts
Prepare trial balances, general ledgers, and financial statements
Complete and submit tax remittance and pension contribution forms
Prepare tax returns and personal bookkeeping services
Compile financial, statistical, and accounting reports
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in accounting or bookkeeping
Or partial completion of a recognized professional accounting program plus relevant experience
Several years of experience as a financial or accounting clerk may substitute for formal education
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 116,700 new positions for Accounting Technicians and Bookkeepers, compared with 108,000 job seekers—making this one of the strongest administrative-finance occupations for overseas professionals planning Canadian immigration.
additional_information: Accounting technicians and bookkeepers are employed across private companies, accounting firms, public institutions, retail businesses, and financial service providers nationwide.

Persistent hiring across all economic sectorsStrong post-pandemic recovery in finance and compliance rolesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $32,800 to $77,600 CAD
